title: Sequential Multi-Modal Fusion

runtime.expected_outcome: Across a 3-step trajectory, the agent integrates multi-channel evidence step by step. Posterior entropy curve shows compounding information gain.

math.latex: q(s_t \mid o^{1}_{1:t}, o^{2}_{1:t}, \ldots) \propto q(s_t \mid o^{1}_{1:t}) \cdot \prod_{k \ge 2} \frac{q(s_t \mid o^k_{1:t})}{q(s_t)}

symbols.factorisation: posterior is the prior times per-channel likelihood ratios

audiences.kid: Every step you see and hear something new. Over time the clues pile up and you become very sure.
audiences.real: Across a trajectory, each channel contributes its own compounding likelihood. The total information gain = sum of per-channel mutual informations.
audiences.equation: Log posterior accumulates log-likelihood ratios across both channels and time. Observation-time factorisation is the key enabler.
audiences.derivation: Under conditional independence across channels and time, the log posterior decomposes into independent contributions. Standard factor-graph belief propagation applies (Bishop 2006, Ch 8).

orchestrate.objective: Trace compounding multi-channel information gain over a trajectory.
orchestrate.context: frog_pond; run a 3-step trajectory and log per-step posterior entropy.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
Bishop (2006), Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning.
